# dotfiles

> Personal dotfiles managed by chezmoi. Bash-based CLI utilities use options.bash for option parsing and rich terminal output. Targets Ubuntu (Debian), Red Hat-like distros (Fedora, RHEL, CentOS, Rocky, Alma), and macOS.

## Chezmoi config

Template data from `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl`.

Top-level keys:
- `osFamily` — `linux` or `darwin`
- `codespaces` — bool, running in GitHub Codespaces
- `hasGui` — bool, machine has GUI
- `name`, `email`, `githubUsername` — user identity

Sniffed values under `.detect.*` (from the detection library at `~/.local/libs/detect-distro.sh`; see `dev-docs/bootstrap-detection-design.md`):
- `.detect.pkgmgr` — `apt`, `dnf`, `pacman`, `zypper`, `apk`, `brew`, …
- `.detect.family` — `debian`, `rhel`, `arch`, `suse`, `darwin`, …
- `.detect.id`, `.detect.idLike` — raw os-release identifiers
- `.detect.versionId`, `.detect.name`, `.detect.arch`, `.detect.uname`
- `.detect.initSystem`, `.detect.isImmutable`, `.detect.isContainer`, `.detect.isWsl`
- `.detect.hasIpv6`, `.detect.sudoGroup`, `.detect.canSudoNopasswd`
- `.detect.hasBrew`, `.detect.hasFlatpak`, `.detect.hasSnap`, `.detect.hasNix`

## Entry points for fresh machines

Two layers, both end in `chezmoi init --apply uhop/dotfiles`.

### install.sh (curl-pipe)

`install.sh` at the repo root is the authoritative bootstrap entry:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/uhop/dotfiles/main/install.sh | sh
# unattended:
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/uhop/dotfiles/main/install.sh | sh -s -- --yes
```

POSIX `sh` (so it works on Alpine / minimal images). Capability-probes `apt-get` / `dnf` / `pacman` / `zypper` / `apk` / `uname -s = Darwin` to route prereq install — **not** ID-based case statements (same principle as the detect library: pkgmgr presence drives behavior, ID is at most a banner hint). Installs curl + git + bash-4 + build tools if missing, then Homebrew (non-interactive), then chezmoi, then runs `chezmoi init --apply`. Flags: `-y/--yes`, `--repo owner/repo`, `--branch <br>`, `-h/--help`.

### bootstrap-dotfiles (thin wrapper in bin/)

`bootstrap-dotfiles` is a ~170-line wrapper around `install.sh` for two flows the curl-pipe doesn't cover:

- **Remote provisioning:** `bootstrap-dotfiles <host>` — checks SSH reachability, then `ssh <host> "curl -fsSL <raw>/install.sh | sh -s -- --yes"`. One command from the operator's box.
- **Self-bootstrap with secrets:** `bootstrap-dotfiles --from-jot <prefix>` — pre-flights that `~/.env` + an age decryption key are on local disk, downloads + runs `install.sh` locally unattended, then invokes `jot-deploy <prefix>` to pull S3-hosted system files.

All OS-specific logic lives in `install.sh`; the wrapper owns only the surrounding flow (SSH check, jot pre-flight, jot-deploy tail).

## Bootstrap: bootstrap.sh

`~/.local/libs/bootstrap.sh` auto-updates options.bash and sources core modules:

```bash
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/ansi.sh
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/args.sh
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/args-version.sh
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/args-help.sh
```

Defines `echoRun` / `echoRun --bold` (colored command echo + eval).
Aliases `doas` as `sudo` when available.

## CLI utilities (options.bash-based)

All source `~/.local/libs/bootstrap.sh`. Pattern:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euCo pipefail
shopt -s expand_aliases
. ~/.local/libs/bootstrap.sh
args::program "$script_name" "1.0" "Description"
args::option "-v, --version" "Show version"
args::option "-h, --help" "Show help"
args::parse "$@"
set -- "${args_cleaned[@]}"
```

### arx

Archive viewer/extractor.

Commands:
- `arx archive.tar.gz` — list contents (default)
- `arx list archive.tar.gz` / `arx l archive.tar.gz` — list contents
- `arx extract archive.tar.gz` — extract to current directory

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Supported formats: `.tar.br`, `.tar.bz2`, `.tar.gz`, `.tar.xz`, `.tar.zst`, `.tar`, `.tbz2`, `.tgz`, `.7z`, `.deb`, `.rar`, `.zip`, `.br`, `.bz2`, `.gz`, `.xz`, `.zst`, `.Z`

### Playbash Server Management

Multi-host bash playbook runner (v1–v3 complete). Replaced the previous Ansible-based stack. See `dev-docs/playbash-design.md`.

**Runner:** Node CLI at `~/.local/bin/playbash` (single entrypoint + modules under `~/.local/share/playbash/`: `runner.js`, `render.js`, `inventory.js`, `sidecar.js`, `staging.js`, `transfer.js`, `commands.js`, `doctor.js`, `errors.js`, `paths.js`, `shell-escape.js`, `subprocess.js`, `ssh-config.js`, `completion.bash`). Cross-platform PTY allocation via `~/.local/libs/playbash-wrap.py` (Python, with stdin relay for `--sudo`). Linux + macOS targets supported end-to-end.

**Subcommands** (targets always come first):
- `playbash run <targets> <playbook>` — run with live rectangle (single target) or status board (multiple targets)
- `playbash push <targets> <script-path>` — upload a local script and run it (always uploads)
- `playbash debug <targets> <playbook>` — same as `run` with `-n 0` and verbose summary
- `playbash exec <targets> [--] <command...>` — one-shot command, no playbook
- `playbash put <targets> <local-path> [<remote-path>]` — upload a file or directory
- `playbash get <targets> <remote-path> [<local-path>]` — download a file or directory
- `playbash list` / `playbash hosts` / `playbash log [host] [command]`

**Options:** `-n/--lines N` (rectangle height, default 5; 0 for exec), `-p/--parallel N`, `--self`, `--sudo` (prompt once for a password; for run/exec/push/debug injects via PTY stdin relay; for put/get wraps remote commands with `sudo -S` and prepends the password to stdin), `--report` (write plain-text summary of actionable events to stdout; for run/push only), `-N/--no-precheck` (skip offline detection).

**Targets:** comma-separated host names, group names, the implicit `all` group, and the special `@self` pseudo-host (resolves to local hostname, implies `--self`). Resolved from `~/.config/playbash/inventory.json`. Inventory hosts are **managed** (playbooks pre-deployed via chezmoi); bare ssh aliases get the playbook pushed automatically. `put`/`get`/`push` support `{host}` template expansion in paths. Custom script paths (containing `/`) in `run`/`push` always use upload mode.

**Offline detection:** pre-flight `ssh -o ConnectTimeout=2 true` parallelized across all targets. Offline hosts get a distinct status, excluded from failure counts. `-N` to skip.

**Reporting model:** playbooks emit JSON-lines events into a per-run sidecar (`$PLAYBASH_REPORT`). Two helper libraries: `~/.local/libs/playbash.sh` (`playbash_info`/`warn`/`error`/`action`/`reboot`/`step`) and `~/.local/libs/maintenance.sh` (`report_reboot`/`report_warn`/`report_action`). Both pretty-print to stderr when `$PLAYBASH_REPORT` is unset.

**Logs:** at `~/.cache/playbash/runs/<host>/<command>/<timestamp>.log`. `playbash log [host] [command]` finds the most recent.

**Periodic tasks:** each managed host runs its own timer via `setup-periodic`. The primary scheduled command is `playbash run @self daily --report` (local run, no SSH). Notification wrappers handle failure emails. See `dev-docs/periodic-tasks-design.md`.

### setup-periodic

Set up periodic scheduled tasks. Creates systemd timers (Linux) or launchd system daemons (macOS).

Commands:
- `setup-periodic add daily "4:00 mo-sa" "playbash run @self daily --report"` — create daily timer
- `setup-periodic add weekly "4:00 su" "playbash run @self weekly --report"` — create weekly timer
- `setup-periodic list` — show active periodic tasks
- `setup-periodic remove daily` — remove a task

Schedule format: `HH:MM`, `Nh`, or `Nm`, optionally followed by day spec (`mo-fr`, `tu,th`, `1,15`).

Options:
- `--email ADDRESS` — notification email (default: chezmoi-configured email; with `add`)
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Prerequisites: requires `sendmail` (msmtp). Fails with install instructions if missing.

Fleet-wide setup: `playbash exec all -- setup-periodic add daily "4:00 mo-sa" "playbash run @self daily --report"`

### Notification wrappers

**notify-playbash** — runs `playbash <args> --report`, emails if exit non-zero OR report non-empty. Silent on clean success. Used by `setup-periodic` for playbash tasks.

**notify-on-failure** — generic wrapper for launchd/cron. Runs a command, captures output, emails on non-zero exit. Silent on success.

**notify-systemd-failure** — systemd `OnFailure=` handler. Called by `email-notify@.service` template unit. Reads `journalctl --user-unit` for the failed unit and emails via `sendmail`.

**email-notify@.service** — systemd template unit at `~/.config/systemd/user/`. Wired via `OnFailure=email-notify@%n.service` on generic task services. Linux only.

### upd

System updater. Template: `executable_upd.tmpl`.

Options:
- `-c, --clean` — run `cln` after updating
- `-y, --yes` — assume yes to all prompts
- `-r, --restart-services` — after a `docker-ce`/`containerd`/`docker-buildx-plugin`/`docker-compose-plugin` upgrade, run `sudo systemctl restart containerd && sudo systemctl restart docker` to recover without a full reboot (falls back to a reboot recommendation if either restart fails). NOPASSWD whitelist entries for these commands ship in `run_onchange_after_install-sudoers.sh.tmpl` (and `run_onchange_after_install-doas.sh.tmpl` if doas is installed).
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Updates: `apt` (Debian), `dnf` (Red Hat-like), `snap` (Debian), `flatpak`, `softwareupdate` (macOS), `mas` (macOS), `brew`, `tmux` plugins, `bun`.

Sources `~/.local/libs/maintenance.sh` for `report_*` helpers and apt-history scanning. After the apt step, `maintenance::check_apt_since` diffs `/var/log/apt/history.log` since the pre-step snapshot and reacts: docker-related upgrades → restart-or-reboot, AppArmor upgrades → eager `aa-remove-unknown` + recovery marker file at `~/.cache/playbash/needs-aa-cleanup`, `/run/reboot-required` → `report_reboot` with the package list as the reason. All events flow into `$PLAYBASH_REPORT` when running under `playbash`.

### cln

System cleanup. Template: `executable_cln.tmpl`.

Options:
- `-y, --yes` — assume yes to all prompts
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Cleans: `apt autoremove/autoclean` (Debian), `dnf autoremove` + `dnf clean all` (Red Hat-like), `flatpak uninstall --unused`, `brew autoremove/cleanup`, `docker system prune`, node version management.

### flatpak-install

Install flatpak packages, dispatching between `--system` and `--user` scopes.

Usage: `flatpak-install [options] <package>...`

Options:
- `--system` — force system install (errors if not viable)
- `--user` — force user install
- `--remote=REMOTE` — flatpak remote to install from (default: `flathub`)
- `-n, --dry-run` — print the plan without installing
- `--verbose` — show per-package dispatch decisions
- `-y, --yes` — assume yes to flatpak prompts
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Dispatch:
1. Dedup via `flatpak info <pkg>` — packages already installed in either scope are skipped.
2. Scope = `system` when the managed polkit rule at `/usr/local/share/polkit-1/rules.d/90-flatpak-ssh.rules` is present AND the user is in `sudo`/`wheel`, OR when `sudo -n true` succeeds.
3. Otherwise scope = `user` (no privileges needed).
4. When scope = `system` without the polkit rule, the install is wrapped in `sudo -n` so it bypasses polkit via root rather than tripping a polkit prompt that can't be answered over SSH.

Probes live under `detect::flatpak_*` in `~/.local/libs/detect-distro.sh` (absorbed from the former standalone library 2026-04-17): `detect::flatpak_has_polkit_rule`, `flatpak_in_sudo_group`, `flatpak_can_system`, `flatpak_system_needs_sudo`, `flatpak_chosen_scope`, `flatpak_scope_of`, `flatpak_has_remote`. `detect::can_sudo_nopasswd` is the shared capability used alongside them.

### Bootstrap detection library

Location: `.chezmoitemplates/detect-distro.sh` + `.chezmoitemplates/detect-packages.sh` — reusable chezmoi template fragments deployed at `~/.local/libs/detect-*.sh` (via thin target `.tmpl` wrappers) and sourced into `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` (via `output "bash" "-c" (includeTemplate ...)`) and `run_onchange_*` scripts (via `{{ template ... }}`). Single source of truth for OS identity + capability detection + package-resolution. Full design in [`dev-docs/bootstrap-detection-design.md`](./dev-docs/bootstrap-detection-design.md).

The library is capability-driven — declared family (`DETECTED_FAMILY`) is a diagnostic label, never a router. Package operations are driven by sniffed package-manager presence + per-capability candidate tables.

#### Sourcing

```bash
. ~/.local/libs/detect-distro.sh
. ~/.local/libs/detect-packages.sh
detect::apply_overrides      # one-shot; applies §3.8 decision table
detect::pkg_ensure c_toolchain git jq   # resolves and installs as needed
```

Both files are idempotent (`__DETECT_DISTRO_LOADED`, `__DETECT_PACKAGES_LOADED` guards). No side effects on sourcing beyond populating the manager registry and candidate tables.

#### Section 1 — Identity (name-based)

- `detect::identity` — populates `DETECTED_ID`, `DETECTED_ID_LIKE`, `DETECTED_VERSION_ID`, `DETECTED_VARIANT_ID`, `DETECTED_NAME`, `DETECTED_FAMILY` from `$OS_RELEASE_PATH` (default `/etc/os-release`). Memoized.
- `detect::family_contains <token>` — 0 if token appears as a whole word in `ID` or `ID_LIKE`. Don't use first-word-of-`ID_LIKE` heuristics.
- `detect::is_version_at_least <n>` — numeric compare on first integer component of `VERSION_ID`.

#### Section 2 — Capabilities (sniffed)

- `detect::has_cmd <cmd>` — memoized PATH check via `detect::_which` indirection.
- `detect::arch`, `detect::uname_s`, `detect::init_system` (systemd|openrc|runit|s6|launchd|unknown).
- `detect::is_immutable` — rpm-ostree / transactional-update / `/run/ostree-booted`.
- `detect::is_container`, `detect::is_wsl`.
- `detect::has_ipv6`, `detect::can_reach <url>`.
- `detect::sudo_group` (wheel|sudo|admin|empty), `detect::can_sudo_nopasswd`.
- `detect::pkgmgr`, `detect::pkgmgrs_present` — priority-ordered sniff (rpm-ostree > transactional-update > apt > dnf > zypper > pacman > apk > …).
- `detect::has_brew`, `detect::has_flatpak`, `detect::has_snap`, `detect::has_nix` + language-scoped `has_{npm_global,pip_user,uv_tool,pipx,cargo,go_install}`.
- `detect::family_consistency` — cross-check declared family against sniffed pkgmgrs (`consistent`|`inconsistent`|`unknown`). Diagnostic only; never blocks.

#### Section 3 — Package resolution

Manager registry (per-manager command templates with `{pkg}` / `{pkgs}` substitution):
- `detect::mgr_register <mgr> <avail> <has> <version> <install>` — register or override a manager's templates.
- Default registrations cover apt, dnf, zypper, pacman, apk, brew, rpm-ostree, transactional-update.

Single-package probes:
- `detect::pkg_avail <mgr> <pkg>` — is `<pkg>` in `<mgr>`'s index?
- `detect::pkg_has <mgr> <pkg>` — is `<pkg>` currently installed via `<mgr>`?
- `detect::pkg_version <mgr> <pkg>` — raw version string (may include packaging tail).
- `detect::pkg_meets <mgr> <pkg> <min_version>` — avail + normalized version comparison.
- `detect::pkg_install <mgr> <pkg…>` — run the install template with the package list batched into one call.

Candidate resolution:
- `detect::active_managers` — sniffed primary managers + brew/nix + optional snap (`DETECT_ALLOW_SNAP=1`), minus anything vetoed by `DETECT_OPT_OUT` or `apply_overrides`.
- `detect::pkg_resolve <capability>` — walks `__DETECT_CANDIDATES[<capability>]` and echoes the first `<mgr> <name>` tuple that resolves.
- `detect::pkg_ensure [--dry-run] [--strict] <capability…>` — resolve each capability, skip already-installed, group remaining by manager, one install call per group.

Decision-table overrides (design §3.8):
- `detect::apply_overrides` — idempotent; disables dnf on Silverblue (rhel + immutable + rpm-ostree), zypper on MicroOS (suse + immutable + transactional-update), warns once on unknown family + present pkgmgr.
- `detect::should_skip_systemd` — container with `init_system=unknown`.
- `detect::should_force_ipv4` — no IPv6 route → consumer curl/wget should pass `-4`.

Internals:
- `detect::_version_normalize <raw>` — strips epoch (`1:`), Debian/RPM packaging suffixes (`-1ubuntu1`, `-3.fc39`), pre-release markers (`~rc1`, `-beta3`), build metadata (`+build4`). MVP — no SemVer pre-release ordering; single seam for a future port (`private_dot_local/private_share/utils/semver.js`).
- `detect::_version_compare <a> <b>` — component-wise numeric compare. Missing trailing components treated as 0.

#### Section 4 — Diagnostics

- `detect::summary` — multi-line human-readable probe snapshot (for debugging; `chezmoi data` consumes `report_json` instead).
- `detect::report_json` — same state as a flat JSON object (for `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` to ingest via `output` → `.detect.*` data keys).
- `detect::reset` — clears memoization + applied overrides. Test-only escape hatch.
- `detect::_assert_no_sudo` — falsifiability guard: library must not be invoked via sudo during sniffing.

#### Candidate tables (`detect-packages.sh`)

Format: `__DETECT_CANDIDATES[<capability>]` is a newline-separated list of `mgr:name[:min_version]` tuples. Blank lines and `#` comments are ignored. Consumers add or override entries freely before calling `pkg_resolve`/`pkg_ensure`. Starter entries: `c_toolchain`, `micro_editor`, `git`, `jq`, `lxd`, `kubectl`.

#### Tests

`bash tests/detect/run-tests.sh` discovers and runs every `test_*.sh` in `tests/detect/`. 12 files today — identity across all 11-distro os-release fixtures, capabilities, pkgmgr, version, pkg_probes, install, resolver, ensure, overrides, network, diagnostics, privilege. Tests stub `detect::_which`, `detect::_run`, `detect::_net_get`, `detect::_user` indirection points for deterministic execution.

### jot

Encrypted S3 notes editor.

Commands:
- `jot` — list files (default)
- `jot file.md.gz.age` / `jot edit file` / `jot e file` — edit file
- `jot list [prefix]` / `jot l [prefix]` — list files
- `jot get file` / `jot g file` — download to current directory
- `jot put local_file remote_name` / `jot p ...` — upload file
- `jot delete file` / `jot del file` / `jot rm file` / `jot d file` — delete
- `jot rename old new` / `jot mv old new` / `jot r old new` — rename

Options:
- `-x, --exact` — exact file name match (not prefix)
- `-y, --yes` — create new file without confirmation
- `-n, --no` — don't create new file
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Environment variables:
- `JOT_AWS_S3_BUCKET` — AWS bucket name (required)
- `JOT_AGE_KEY` — age key path (default: `~/.ssh/age.key`)
- `JOT_SSH_PUB` — SSH public key (default: auto-detected — first of `id_ed25519.pub`, `id_rsa.pub`, `id_ecdsa.pub`)
- `JOT_SSH_PRV` — SSH private key (default: auto-detected — first of `id_ed25519`, `id_rsa`, `id_ecdsa`)

Supported codecs: `.age`, `.br`, `.bz2`, `.gz`, `.xz`, `.zst`

### goup

Run a command in the current directory and each parent directory up to `/`.

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Usage: `goup "make test"` — runs `make test` in `.`, `..`, `../..`, etc.

### ollama-sync

Update all installed ollama models.

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

### mount-raid

Mount a remote NFS filesystem. Template: `executable_mount-raid.tmpl`.

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Usage: `mount-raid server-name`

## CLI utilities (standalone)

### gpurr

Git pull with submodule recursion + pull main branch in each submodule.

### gpwiki

Git pull + pull main in `wiki/` submodule if present.

### update-dependencies

Clone or update `options.bash` sparse worktree at `~/.local/share/libs/scripts`.

### git-br

Pretty-formatted `git branch` with date, author, subject.

### git-brr

Remote branches sorted by committer date (`git-br --remote --sort=-committerdate`).

### git-bs

Branch list sorted by author date in columnar format.

### git-mbs

Filter `git-bs --all` to show only branches by the current git user.

### git-pull-main

Checkout and pull the default branch (auto-detected from `origin`).

### git-super-clean

`git reset --hard` + `git clean --force -d -x`.

## Shell aliases (dot_bash_aliases)

Key aliases and functions:

### File listing (eza)
- `l` → `eza`
- `ls` → `eza --grid --color auto --icons --sort=type`
- `ll` → `eza --long ...`
- `la` → `eza --grid --all ...`
- `lla` → `eza --long --all ...`
- `ltr` → `eza -l -t modified -r`
- `l.` → list dotfiles only

### Navigation (zoxide)
- `..` → `z ..`
- `...` → `z ../../`
- `up N` → go up N directories
- `zl [dir]` → `z dir && l`
- `mkz dir` → `mkdir dir && z dir`

### File operations
- `rm` → `rm -I --preserve-root`
- `mv` → `mv -iv`
- `cp` → `cp -iv`
- `rcp` / `rmv` → rsync copy/move with aggregate progress
- `rup` / `rsy` → rsync update / sync

### Git
- `gst` → `git status`
- `gco` → `git checkout`
- `gcob` → `git checkout -b`
- `gcm` → `git commit`
- `gbr` → `git branch`
- `gpull` → `git pull`
- `gpush` → `git push`
- `gsw` → `git switch` (default branch if no args)
- `gme` → `git merge` (default branch if no args)

### Better replacements
- `cat` → `bat` / `batcat`
- `ping` → `sudo prettyping`
- `top` → `sudo htop`
- `du` → `ncdu`
- `help` → `tldr`

### SSH helpers
- `ssht host [session]` → SSH + tmux attach/new
- `mosht host [session]` → mosh + tmux
- `ett host [session]` → Eternal Terminal + tmux
- `kssh` → `kitten ssh`
- `kssht host [session]` → kitten ssh + tmux

### Other
- `where "pattern" [path/wildcard]` — recursive grep in files
- `upfind` / `upfd` — search up directory tree
- `upsearch` → `upfd -gH` (no glob expansion)
- `tre` → `tree` excluding common junk dirs
- `gre` → `grep -r` excluding common junk dirs
- `oports` → list open ports
- `psr` / `psm` / `psg` / `pst` — process listing variants
- `nowrap [col] [-col]` — truncate output to terminal width

## Environment

Set in `dot_bashrc`:
- `EDITOR=micro`
- `VISUAL=micro`
- `FORCE_COLOR=3`
- `GITHUB_USERNAME` — rendered from the chezmoi `githubUsername` data value; exported before `~/.env` is sourced so user `.env` can override
- `JOT_AWS_S3_BUCKET=${GITHUB_USERNAME}-notes` — exported only if unset and `GITHUB_USERNAME` is non-empty (so `~/.env` can pin a custom bucket; users without a GitHub identity don't get a bogus `-notes` default)

## Links

- Chezmoi: https://www.chezmoi.io/
- options.bash: https://github.com/uhop/options.bash
- options.bash wiki: https://github.com/uhop/options.bash/wiki
